docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: System overview
docs.text:
Arqon ContextOS connects GPT role agents to repo-backed context and artifact storage.

PM / Coder / Auditor GPTs
  -> GPT Action
  -> Cloudflare Worker broker
  -> GitHub App installation token
  -> private project repos
  -> generated context files and run artifacts

docs.title: 1. Cloudflare Worker broker
docs.text: The broker authenticates GPT Action calls, enforces role permissions, maps projects to repos, and reads/writes only allowlisted governance paths.

docs.title: 2. GitHub App
docs.text: The GitHub App provides scoped repository access. It should be installed only on repos in the same security domain.

docs.title: 3. Project context package
docs.text:
Each project repo contains:

  • governance/context/
  • governance/runs/
  • governance/messages/
  • governance/notes/
  • governance/ledger/
  • scripts/build_gpt_context.py
  • .github/workflows/build-gpt-context.yml

docs.title: 4. GPT commands
docs.text:
The GPTs use instruction-level commands that map to broker endpoints:

  • /sync-constitution
  • /sync-context
  • /save-context
  • /send-message
  • /inbox
  • /open-message
  • /create-run
  • /load-run
  • /write-artifact
  • /checkpoint

docs.title: Authority model
docs.text:
PM proposes. Coder implements. Helper executes. Auditor verifies. Human promotes.

No AI self-certifies.

docs.title: Cloudflare Deployment
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Cloudflare account
  • Wrangler installed
  • GitHub App created and installed on target repo/security domain
  • Arqon ContextOS Worker configured

docs.title: Worker secrets
docs.text:
Set these as Cloudflare Worker secrets, not repo files:

  • GITHUB_APP_ID
  • GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID
  • GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
  • BROKER_KEY_PM
  • BROKER_KEY_CODER
  • BROKER_KEY_AUDITOR
  • BROKER_KEY_HELPER
  • BROKER_KEY_HUMAN

docs.title: Local commands
docs.text:
From worker/:

npm install
npm run typecheck
npm run dev
npm run deploy

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
The initial Worker is a v0.1 broker scaffold. Review and test thoroughly before using it on private repos.

No secrets should be committed.

docs.title: GitHub App Setup
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Recommended app model
docs.text:
Use one GitHub App per security domain.

For Arqon Zero private infrastructure, create:

Arqon ContextOS Governance App

docs.title: Repository permissions
docs.text:
Start with:

  • Contents: Read-only for read broker
  • Contents: Read/write only after write endpoints are enabled
  • Metadata: Read-only

Optional later:

  • Pull requests: Read/write
  • Issues: Read/write

docs.title: Install target
docs.text:
Install only on selected repos, such as:

  • novelbytelabs/ArqonZero

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text:
Store GitHub App credentials only as Cloudflare Worker secrets.

Do not commit private keys, installation tokens, webhook secrets, or personal access tokens.

docs.title: GPT Commands
docs.text:
Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

These are instruction-level commands. They trigger GPT Actions to the Arqon ContextOS broker.

docs.title: /sync-constitution
docs.text:
Load current shared and role constitution.

Example: /sync-constitution project=ArqonZero role=PM_AI

docs.title: /sync-context
docs.text:
Load current project context for the role.

Example: /sync-context project=ArqonZero role=CODER_AI

docs.title: /save-context
docs.text:
Save a GPT response or idea as a non-official context note.

Example: /save-context project=ArqonZero role=CODER_AI title="Claim guard ideas" tags=governance,deception

docs.title: /send-message
docs.text:
Send a message from one role inbox to another.

Example: /send-message project=ArqonZero from=CODER_AI to=PM_AI subject="Review claim guard idea"

docs.title: /inbox
docs.text:
List unread role messages.

Example: /inbox project=ArqonZero role=PM_AI

docs.title: /open-message
docs.text:
Open a message.

Example: /open-message project=ArqonZero role=PM_AI message_id=MSG-2026-0001

docs.title: /create-run
docs.text:
Create a run folder and manifest.

Example: /create-run project=ArqonZero title="Add claim-language guard" owner=PM_AI

docs.title: /load-run
docs.text:
Load current run state and role-relevant artifacts.

Example: /load-run project=ArqonZero run_id=AZ-2026-0001 role=AUDITOR_AI

docs.title: /write-artifact
docs.text:
Write an official role artifact.

Example: /write-artifact project=ArqonZero run_id=AZ-2026-0001 type=pm_spec

docs.title: /checkpoint
docs.text:
Save session state as a context note.

Example: /checkpoint project=ArqonZero role=PM_AI title="End of morning PM session"

docs.title: Project Integration Guide
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Integrate a project repo with Arqon ContextOS.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Copy project_templates/ contents into the project repo.
  2. Edit governance/context/project_context.yaml.
  3. Run python3 scripts/build_gpt_context.py.
  4. Run python3 scripts/verify_context_manifest.py.
  5. Commit generated context files.
  6. Enable .github/workflows/build-gpt-context.yml.
  7. Add the repo to the ContextOS broker project map.
  8. Test /sync-context.

docs.title: Required project files
docs.text:
• governance/context/current_context_snapshot.json
  • governance/context/context_manifest.json
  • governance/context/context_ledger.jsonl
  • governance/context/pm_gpt_context.json
  • governance/context/coder_gpt_context.json
  • governance/context/auditor_gpt_context.json

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Phase 0
docs.text: Create ArqonContextOS repo and docs.

docs.title: Phase 1
docs.text: Create project-side context templates.

docs.title: Phase 2
docs.text: Install templates into ArqonZero.

docs.title: Phase 3
docs.text: Deploy Cloudflare Worker read broker.

docs.title: Phase 4
docs.text: Update GPT Actions to use broker.

docs.title: Phase 5
docs.text: Add notes and message boxes.

docs.title: Phase 6
docs.text: Add official run artifact writes.

docs.title: Phase 7
docs.text: Add KV cache and GitHub webhook refresh.

docs.title: Phase 8
docs.text: Add hardening: role permission tests, claim-language guard, hash-chain guard, evidence completeness score, and forbidden path tests.

docs.title: Security Model
docs.text: Status: REQUIRES_HUMAN_REVIEW development diagnostic only NOT SEALED-TEST CERTIFIED not promotable

docs.title: Main security controls
docs.text:
1. No arbitrary repo browsing.
  2. No arbitrary path writes.
  3. Separate broker API keys per role.
  4. GitHub App access scoped per security domain.
  5. Generated context files are built inside project repos.
  6. Broker reads/writes only allowlisted paths.
  7. Status labels are mandatory.
  8. Promotion remains human-only.

docs.title: Role keys
docs.text:
Recommended secrets:

  • BROKER_KEY_PM
  • BROKER_KEY_CODER
  • BROKER_KEY_AUDITOR
  • BROKER_KEY_HELPER
  • BROKER_KEY_HUMAN

docs.title: Role write permissions
docs.text:
PM AI may write PM specs, PM task packets, PM notes, PM messages, and run creation events.

Coder AI may write coder patch bundles, coder handoffs, coder notes, and coder messages.

Helper/Codex may write helper reports, logs, and evidence manifests.

Auditor AI may write audit reports, audit scores, claim audits, and auditor notes/messages.

Human may write human decisions, exception manifests, and promotion manifests.

docs.title: Forbidden paths
docs.text:
The broker must never write or return these paths:

  • .env
  • secrets/
  • sealed/
  • holdout/
  • models/
  • data/
  • private/
  • credentials/

For v0.1, broker writes to src/, tests/, and .github/ are forbidden.
